rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant. Mr. Aahl¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Mazda North American Operations¿ motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed July 7. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Lone Star Industries, Inc.¿s amended motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Nelson was exposed to insulag or any other asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Nelson was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES RAGAN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Huebsch Services¿ motion to quash service of summons and first amended complaint is continued to October 5, 2011 to allow plaintiffs to conduct discovery on the issue of personal jurisdiction. Supplemental opposition papers, if any, to be filed and served by September 23, 2011. Supplemental reply papers, if any, to be filed and served by September 30, 2011. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 14, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Huebsch Services¿ demurrer to first amended complaint is overruled. The demurrer is based on asserted facts which are neither alleged in the complaint nor are subject to judicial notice. Defendant need not respond further to the complaint unless and until its motion to quash is denied. = (220/HEK)
